titleEn: Many growers still find ‘no meaningful benefit’ from AI use on the farm: survey
titleZh: 调查显示多数农民认为AI在农场“无实质益处”
summaryZh: 普渡大学/CME集团农业经济晴雨表最新调查显示，52%的美国农民认为AI和数据驱动工具目前对其经营“无实质益处”，仅14%认为能减少劳动力需求，22.8%认为能提高产量。调查还指出高投入成本是农场财务健康的最大制约因素（42%）。尽管专家认为随着时间推移农民会逐渐发现AI的价值，但当前采纳障碍包括执行难度和外部因素（如天气、设备故障）。
featuredReason: 该调查直接反映了农业（包括畜牧与种植）一线生产者对AI技术的真实态度，揭示了技术推广中的核心痛点——农民认为AI“无实质益处”，这对智慧畜牧行业从业者理解用户需求、调整产品策略具有重要参考价值。
contentFull: The agtech community often cites AI and digital tools as ways to help farmers cut costs, particularly around labor and inputs. But farmers surveyed by Purdue still express significant skepticism when it comes to implementing and using such tools, according to the most recent Purdue University/CME Group Ag Economy Barometer. The Ag Economy Barometer calculates data from US agricultural producers that participate in a survey each month. More than half (52%) of the 400 farmers surveyed said AI and data-driven tools provide “no meaningful benefit” to their operation currently. Where farmers do see benefits, they aren’t necessarily in line with agtech’s frequent narrative around labor savings. Just 14% thought AI and/or data-driven tools would reduce labor needs and costs on the farm. A slightly larger number (22.8%) believe these tools will increase production, while AI and digitization are seen as not having much benefit at all when it comes to reducing risk and uncertainty. Michael Langemeier, director at the CCA and principle investigator for the Barometer, suggested in a recent video that this sentiment will likely change over time. “I think there will be meaningful benefits, but at least at this point, the majority of respondents really haven’t figured out how the AI or data-driven tools [are] going to help their operation.” He noted this is the first time the Barometer has asked questions around AI and digitization. In future, he said, “there will be a higher percent that believes these tools are going to benefit their operation.” Whether farmers can actually follow recommendations made by AI and digital tools was another question, and one inherently connected to external forces, or as Langemeier puts it, “facts on the ground.” An AI-enabled tools could recommend planting corn on a certain day, but weather or broken equipment could make it impossible to follow that exact recommendation, for example. “There’s going to be a learning tool curve with these data-driven tools, and it may be difficult to follow the prescription precisely,” he noted. Elsewhere in the Barometer, 42% of farmers cited high input costs the biggest constraint to improving their farm’s financial health. Low output prices ranked second at 17%, followed by weather risk at 14%, policy uncertainty at 11%, labor and equipment concerns at 9%, and debt or financial pressure at 8%. According to Langemeier, “While high input costs remain the primary constraint on farm financial performance, producers are continuing to make decisions in a broader environment shaped by technology adoption, trade expectations and long-term land value outlook.” Share this article Sign up for our weekly newsletter. AgriFood Signals: Uber’s $14.8bn bid for Delivery Hero, Hippo Harvest banks $30m, agentic AI for ag Agtech FoodTech Rize lands $31m to help rice farmers cut methane emissions, water usage Agtech APAC
translationZh:
农业科技界常将AI和数字工具视为帮助农民降低成本（尤其是劳动力和投入成本）的途径。但根据普渡大学/CME集团最新发布的农业经济晴雨表，受访农民在实施和使用此类工具时仍表现出显著的怀疑态度。

农业经济晴雨表每月对美国农业生产者进行调查并计算数据。在400名受访农民中，超过半数（52%）表示，AI和数据驱动工具目前对其经营“无实质益处”。在农民确实看到益处的方面，也未必与农业科技界常说的节省劳动力叙事一致。仅14%的人认为AI和/或数据驱动工具能减少农场的劳动力需求和成本。略多的人（22.8%）认为这些工具能提高产量，而AI和数字化在降低风险与不确定性方面被认为几乎没有益处。

晴雨表负责人兼首席研究员Michael Langemeier在最近的一段视频中表示，这种情绪可能会随时间改变。“我认为未来会有实质性的益处，但至少目前，大多数受访者还没有弄清楚AI或数据驱动工具将如何帮助他们的经营。”他指出这是晴雨表首次就AI和数字化相关问题进行调查。未来，“会有更高比例的人相信这些工具将有益于他们的经营。”

农民能否真正遵循AI和数字工具的建议是另一个问题，且与外部因素（或如Langemeier所说的“地面事实”）密切相关。例如，AI工具可能建议在某天种植玉米，但天气或设备故障可能使精确遵循该建议变得不可能。“这些数据驱动工具会有一个学习曲线，而且可能很难精确遵循处方，”他指出。

晴雨表还显示，42%的农民认为高投入成本是改善农场财务健康的最大制约因素。产出价格低排名第二（17%），其次是天气风险（14%）、政策不确定性（11%）、劳动力和设备问题（9%），以及债务或财务压力（8%）。Langemeier表示：“虽然高投入成本仍然是农场财务表现的主要制约因素，但生产者正在一个由技术采纳、贸易预期和长期土地价值前景塑造的更广泛环境中继续做出决策。”
author: Jennifer Marston
